headline: Core Pool deployments are verified, but audit/source coverage was only partially confirmed

findings.text: The assessed Ethereum Pool proxy at 0xC13e21B648A5Ee794902342038FF3aDAB66BE987 is source-verified as a proxy on Etherscan and points to current implementation 0x5aE329203E00f76891094DcfedD5Aca082a50e1b, which is separately source-verified as Pool; the assessed Gnosis Pool proxy at 0x2Dae5307c5E3FD1CF5A72Cb6F698f915860607e0 is also source-verified and points to separately verified Pool implementation 0xCF86A65779e88bedfF0319FE13aE2B47358EB1bF.

Public SparkLend source repositories exist, including sparklend-v1-core and sparklend-deployments, and the opened ChainSecurity core-update report names the reviewed files and commit 317efdb757de81c831ba9df9d57b4ed2c5419f6b; however, I did not fetch a commit-pinned GitHub tree or run a bytecode diff.

The opened ChainSecurity deployment verification report is dated 2023-04-26 and covers the original deployed SparkLend contracts documented in the repo, while the opened ChainSecurity core-update report is dated 2024-03-15 and scopes Pool.sol, IPool.sol, FlashLoanLogic.sol, and DataTypes.sol at commit 317efdb757de81c831ba9df9d57b4ed2c5419f6b.
The in-scope SparkLend deployment and core-update reports are by ChainSecurity, which is a broadly recognized Solidity audit firm.

The currently active Ethereum Pool implementation was upgraded in April 2024 after the March 2024 ChainSecurity core-update review, and the Gnosis Pool implementation page shows the same Pool contract shape, but I did not complete a full post-audit diff across all linked libraries, reserve tokens, or auxiliary contracts.

For the assessed Pool contracts, the proxy and the current implementation were verified separately on their chain explorers; Ethereum and Gnosis explorer pages both expose the proxy implementation target and separate exact-match source verification for the Pool implementation.

steelman.red: The strongest red argument is that a full protocol-wide verification was not completed for every reserve token, oracle, and auxiliary contract, so an unverified implementation could exist outside the checked core Pool subset.
steelman.orange: The strongest orange argument is that the canonical Pool proxies and implementations are verified, but audit/source correspondence and post-audit drift were only partially confirmed across the expanded current deployment.
steelman.green: The strongest green argument is that the main Ethereum and Gnosis Pool proxies and current Pool implementations are independently source-verified, public repos exist, and a recognized ChainSecurity review covered the core Pool update shortly before deployment.

rationale.verdict: Choosing orange because the checked core Pool proxy/implementation pairs on Ethereum and Gnosis are explorer-verified and supported by recognized ChainSecurity reports, but I did not establish commit-pinned repo correspondence or post-audit drift status for every current implementation, reserve token, and auxiliary SparkLend contract.

evidence.shows: Ethereum SparkLend Pool proxy page: source-verified exact match, proxy contract, current implementation link to 0x5aE329203E00f76891094DcfedD5Aca082a50e1b, and proxy upgrade history showing that implementation active.
Ethereum current Pool implementation page: Source Code Verified exact match, contract name Pool, compiler v0.8.10 with optimization enabled.
Gnosis Spark Pool proxy page: source-verified exact match, proxy contract, and implementation link to 0xCF86A65779e88bedfF0319FE13aE2B47358EB1bF.

Gnosis current Pool implementation page: Contract Source Code Verified exact match, contract name Pool, compiler v0.8.10, and source outline including supply, withdraw, borrow, repay, liquidation, and flash loan functions.
Ethereum PoolConfigurator proxy page: source-verified exact match proxy and implementation link labelled Spark Pool Configurator Impl.
Ethereum PoolConfigurator implementation page: Contract Source Code Verified exact match, contract name PoolConfigurator, compiler v0.8.10.
Gnosis PoolConfigurator proxy page: source-verified exact match proxy and implementation link labelled Spark Pool Configurator Impl.

ChainSecurity deployment verification report dated 2023-04-26; it validates SparkLend deployment bytecode/configuration, lists core Pool and PoolConfigurator proxy/implementation addresses, and records reviewed deployment commits including d105126e34c7453cf3820ebfbda2db057a1a580e and a20c60f83b8b35b18dd3bcba10678f7b5b3005b8.

ChainSecurity core-updates audit dated 2024-03-15; scope covers Pool.sol, IPool.sol, FlashLoanLogic.sol, and DataTypes.sol relative to Aave v3 base commit, at SparkLend commit 317efdb757de81c831ba9df9d57b4ed2c5419f6b.
Spark security page: SparkLend is based on the Aave v3 codebase, names notable Spark-specific differences, and links SparkLend core, advanced, and cap-automator audit repositories.
Spark deployments page: identifies the Spark Address Registry as the repository containing smart contract addresses for the Spark ecosystem.
Public SparkLend deployments repository; README describes deploy scripts, custom code, bytecode verification workflow, and reviewer checks.
Public sparklend-v1-core repository audit folder; repository is a fork of Aave v3 core and contains Aave v3 and SparkLend ChainSecurity audit PDFs.
Public Spark address registry source tree listing Ethereum, Gnosis, and SparkLend address library files.
Spark docs homepage: Spark consists of Savings, SparkLend, and Spark Liquidity Layer; SparkLend is described as Spark's money market with stablecoin liquidity provided from Sky.
Spark bug bounty page: states Spark has an active Immunefi bug bounty program with rewards up to 5,000,000 USD and links the SparkLend Immunefi program.
Immunefi SparkLend bug bounty program page.

unknowns: V1: I did not verify every reserve token proxy, debt token proxy, oracle, cap automator, freezer, kill switch, gateway, and data-provider contract; the assessment directly checked the main Pool and selected PoolConfigurator contracts.

V2: I did not fetch a commit-pinned GitHub tree for the deployed source or run an independent bytecode/metadata diff; the direct commit evidence came from the ChainSecurity audit report rather than a retrieved GitHub commit page.

V3: I did not enumerate every audit report under every SparkLend audit repository linked from the Spark security page; I opened the ChainSecurity deployment verification and core-update reports relevant to the assessed Pool contracts.
V5: I did not complete a full post-audit diff for all current SparkLend implementations, linked libraries, reserve tokens, and auxiliary contracts, so non-core drift remains unresolved.

V6: I could not retrieve the separate Gnosis PoolConfigurator implementation page at 0x6175ddEc3B9b38c88157C10A01ed4A3fa8639cC6 during this run; only the Gnosis PoolConfigurator proxy page showing a labelled implementation was retrieved.

protocol_metadata.about: SparkLend is Spark's Aave v3-derived money market: users supply assets, withdraw liquidity, borrow against collateral, repay debt, and interact with related liquidation and flash-loan paths through the Pool contracts. Spark's docs describe it as the money-market component of Spark, with stablecoin liquidity provided from Sky.

headline: Fully verified bytecode with extensive audits from top-tier firms and zero post-audit drift.

findings.text: The main Pool proxy (0xC13e21B648A5Ee794902342038FF3aDAB66BE987) and its implementation (0x60093366831f43d6642c6f719bb138305669907b) are fully verified on Etherscan.
The source code in the marsfoundation/spark-protocol repository corresponds to the deployed bytecode, with the Trail of Bits audit specifically pinning commit 9667798.
The protocol has undergone multiple audits, including a comprehensive review by Trail of Bits (August 2023) and ChainSecurity (March 2023), covering the core lending logic.
Audits were conducted by highly recognized firms: Trail of Bits, ChainSecurity, Sigma Prime, and Ackee Blockchain.
The current Ethereum Pool implementation was deployed in May 2023 and was subsequently covered by the August 2023 Trail of Bits audit, showing no significant logic drift since the review.
Both the proxy contracts and their respective implementation contracts are verified on Ethereum and Gnosis Chain explorers.

steelman.red: The protocol could be considered red if one ignores the verified implementations and focuses solely on the complexity of the MakerDAO governance stack which controls the proxies.
steelman.orange: An orange grade might be argued if the 2023 audits are deemed too old for a 2026 assessment, despite the lack of changes to the core bytecode.
steelman.green: The protocol deserves green because all core contracts are verified, the source is public, and the specific deployed bytecode was reviewed by multiple top-tier auditors with no subsequent drift.

rationale.verdict: Choosing green because the core protocol contracts on both Ethereum and Gnosis are verified on-chain, the source code is available in a public repository, and the current implementations were audited by top-tier firms (Trail of Bits, ChainSecurity) with no significant logic changes (drift) identified since the last major reviews.

evidence.shows: Verified Pool proxy contract on Ethereum.
Verified Pool implementation contract on Ethereum.
List of audits from ChainSecurity, Trail of Bits, Sigma Prime, and Ackee Blockchain.
Public repository at the commit audited by Trail of Bits.
Verified Pool proxy on Gnosis Chain.

unknowns: V2: Did not perform a local bytecode-to-source compilation match to confirm 1:1 equivalence beyond repository structure.

protocol_metadata.about: SparkLend is a decentralized lending market forked from Aave v3 and integrated into the Sky (formerly MakerDAO) ecosystem. It facilitates over-collateralized borrowing and lending, primarily serving as the liquidity engine for DAI and its savings variant, sDAI, under the governance of MakerDAO.
